annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":57879,"title":"[Core] Docs: Document GlobalDisableAutoRecording and auto-re-recording behavior in Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework` **Service directory:** `sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/` **Triggered by:** PR #57860 (commit 0e47adc) by `@joseharriaga` ## What Changed PR #57860 fixed a bug in `RecordedTestAttribute` where `TestEnvironment.GlobalDisableAutoRecording` (set via the `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` environment variable) was not being checked before attempting to auto-re-record a failing playback test. The fix ensures that when `GlobalDisableAutoRecording` is `true`, the attribute skips the automatic re-record attempt and returns the original failure result immediately. ## Gaps Found - The `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` environment variable is not listed in the README's **Test settings** section, which currently only documents `TestMode` and `TestTimeout` - The auto-re-recording behavior of `[RecordedTest]` is not explained anywhere in the README — users don't know that a failing playback test will automatically attempt a re-record, or how to suppress that behavior - The CHANGELOG does not mention the bug fix (under `### Bugs Fixed` for the unreleased version) 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1:** `sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/CHANGELOG.md` — Edit Add the bug fix entry under `### Bugs Fixed` for the unreleased version: ```markdown ### Bugs Fixed - Fixed `RecordedTestAttribute` to honor `GlobalDisableAutoRecording` when deciding whether to auto-re-record a failing playback test. Previously, the attribute would attempt to re-record even when `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` was set, ignoring the opt-out signal. ``` **File 2:** `sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/README.md` — Edit 1. **Expand the Test settings section** to include `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING`: Replace the existing Test settings block: ```markdown ## Test settings Test settings can be configured via `.runsettings` files. The Test Framework recognizes the following settings: - `TestMode`: Override the test mode (`Live`, `Record`, `Playback`) - `TestTimeout`: Set the global test timeout in seconds ``` With: ```markdown ## Test settings Test settings can be configured via `.runsettings` files or environment variables. The Test Framework recognizes the following settings: - `TestMode` / `CLIENTMODEL_TEST_MODE`: Override the test mode (`Live`, `Record`, `Playback`) - `TestTimeout`: Set the global test timeout in seconds - `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING`: Set to `true` to disable automatic re-recording when a `[RecordedTest]` fails in Playback mode. By default, a playback failure caused by a request mismatch triggers an automatic re-record attempt. Setting this variable suppresses that behavior and surfaces the original failure directly. ``` 2. **Add a subsection under Recorded tests** explaining auto-re-recording behavior. Insert the following after the **Sanitizing** subsection and before **Unit tests**: ```markdown #### Auto-re-recording on playback failure When a `[RecordedTest]` fails in Playback mode due to a request mismatch (i.e., the client issued a request that does not match the recorded session), the `RecordedTestAttribute` automatically switches the test to Record mode and re-runs it against the live service. If the re-record succeeds, the test completes with an **Error** status and a message indicating that the updated recording file should be copied to the `SessionRecords` directory before re-running. To disable this behavior — for example in CI environments where live service access is unavailable — set the `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` environment variable to `true`. When set, playback failures are surfaced as plain failures without any re-record attempt. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for this documentation update. The changes are purely prose additions to the README and CHANGELOG. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains the required sections. The changes above add content to the existing **Test settings** section and the **Recorded tests** subsection — no structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `dotnet format sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/` No snippet or API export steps are required since no new public API surface or snippet regions are added. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24099840433/agentic_workflow) · ● 301.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57879","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-07T19:33:48Z","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:33:48Z"}

If the re-record succeeds, the test completes with an **Error** status and a message indicating that the updated recording file should be copied to the `SessionRecords` directory before re-running. To disable this behavior — for example in CI environments where live service access is unavailable — set the `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` environment variable to `true`. When set, playback failures are surfaced as plain failures without any re-record attempt. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for this documentation update. The changes are purely prose additions to the README and CHANGELOG. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains the required sections. The changes above add content to the existing **Test settings** section and the **Recorded tests** subsection — no structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `dotnet format sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/` No snippet or API export steps are required since no new public API surface or snippet regions are added. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24099840433/agentic_workflow) · ● 301.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57879","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-07T19:33:48Z","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:33:48Z"}

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents a gap in the `Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework` README and CHANGELOG: the `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` environment variable and the auto-re-recording behavior of `[RecordedTest]` are not documented, and the corresponding bug fix (PR #57860) is missing from the CHANGELOG.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework` (unversioned — targets unreleased version)
**Affected API:** `RecordedTestAttribute`, `TestEnvironment.GlobalDisableAutoRecording`
**Scenarios:**
- The `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` env var is absent from the README's **Test settings** section
- The auto-re-recording behavior on playback failure is not documented anywhere in the README
- The bug fix for `RecordedTestAttribute` ignoring `GlobalDisableAutoRecording` is missing from CHANGELOG under `### Bugs Fixed`

**Root ask:** Update the README to document the env var and auto-re-recording behavior, and add a CHANGELOG entry for the bug fix introduced in PR #57860.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The fix landed in PR #57860 (commit `0e47adc`, author `@joseharriaga`). The implementation change is complete; only documentation artifacts remain to be updated.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Open `sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/README.md` and expand the **Test settings** section to include `CLIENTMODEL_DISABLE_AUTO_RECORDING` with a description of its effect on auto-re-recording.
2. Add a subsection under **Recorded tests** explaining the auto-re-recording-on-playback-failure behavior and how to suppress it.
3. Open `sdk/core/Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework/CHANGELOG.md` and add a `### Bugs Fixed` entry under the current unreleased version describing the `RecordedTestAttribute` fix.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — the issue concerns SDK documentation and behavior (test framework code), not a management-plane or provisioning package.
- **Service:** `System.ClientModel` — `Microsoft.ClientModel.TestFramework` lives in `sdk/core/` and is the companion test framework for the `System.ClientModel` / `Microsoft.ClientModel` library family; the CODEOWNERS `%System.ClientModel` entry covers this area.
- **Confidence:** High — the package path, service directory, and CODEOWNERS entry all align unambiguously.
</details>
